commit.message:
feat: Marshall enrichment — regulatory intelligence in AI Review Board

Stage 2 of the Review Board: replaces thin REGULATORY_CONTEXT dict with
comprehensive ENFORCEMENT_INTELLIGENCE database covering all 10 packs.

Each violation now includes:
- Enforcement priority (active/watching/dormant)
- Penalty exposure per violation
- Recent enforcement cases with citations
- Marshall urgency score (0.0–1.0)
- Statutory basis and regulation reference

Response includes marshall_summary with avg urgency, highest risk rule,
and active enforcement count. Enrichment is computed deterministically
(no extra LLM call) — Sprint 10 replaces static data with Marshall's
live RSS cron feed.

Add robustness for density-bounded and large-R_st profiles

- HIIRegion gains integration_points and max_radius parameters:
  integration_points are passed as breakpoints to scipy.integrate.quad,
  fixing convergence for cored profiles where the density transitions at r0;
  max_radius defaults to 300 pc (ISM gas scale height) and raises RuntimeError
  for unphysically large Stromgren radii
- _recomb_rate catches OverflowError (density-bounded medium where total
  recombination rate < Q) and returns inf; _stromgren_radius_numeric detects
  this and raises a descriptive RuntimeError
- Bracket loop converted from unbounded while to 300-step for loop
- plot_cored_profile and report_cored_stromgren updated to include r0=1 pc
  and handle density-bounded cases gracefully

commit.message:
feat(security): detect CVE-2025-9906 Keras enable_unsafe_deserialization config bypass (#601)

Add detection for CVE-2025-9906 (CVSS 8.6): config.json in .keras archives
can reference keras.config.enable_unsafe_deserialization to disable safe_mode
from within the deserialization process itself, then load malicious Lambda
layers. Fixed in Keras 3.11.0.

Detection approach:
- Raw text scan before JSON parsing (catches malformed JSON bypass)
- Structured config scan after parsing (context-aware, requires both
  keras.config context and enable_unsafe_deserialization token in same object)
- Documentation-style content filtering to reduce false positives
- Deduplication between raw and structured detection paths

Co-authored-by: Yash Chhabria <yash2998chhabria@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Generalization refactor: generic step runner, extraction layer, sub-item support

Refactor BaseRunner into a concrete step-based executor that gives new
playbooks resumability, throttling, retries, concurrency, circuit
breaking, and results.csv writing for free — a new playbook only needs
step functions, input schema, and output schema.

- Add StepDefinition, SampleContext, SubItemContext, BaseRunner to
  runners/base.py; rename old abstract class to PlaybookRunner
- Migrate TicketsRunner as proof-of-concept (4 step functions + schemas)
- Add generic extraction layer to adapters/browser.py: ExtractionRule,
  extract_fields(), find_links_matching(), verify_url() via aiohttp
- Add SubItemContext for long-context tasks (1 sample → N sub-items)
  with per-sub-item state, screenshots, skip logic, and notes roundtrip
- Add SubItemNotes to SampleNotes schema in manifest.py
- 224 tests passing (110 new tests across 3 new test files)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(hero): load all 7 panel images eagerly + increase scroll height

lazy loading on horizontally-offset panels means panels 3-7 (at 300-700vw
right of viewport) fall outside the browser's lazy-load proximity threshold
and never fetch. Changed all panel images to loading='eager' (fetchpriority
='low' for panels 1-7 to avoid bandwidth spike).

Also raised tasting-sequence height 550vh → 650vh: each of the 7 scroll
transitions now gets ~78vh instead of ~64vh, giving users more time to read
each panel before it scrolls away.

commit.message:
Plan deprecation of legacy gen_server beamtalk_interface.erl (BT-1093) (#1150)

## Summary

Audit and plan the deprecation of Phase 1 gen_server implementations
(`beamtalk_interface.erl`, `beamtalk_workspace_interface.erl`) in favour
of Phase 2 compiled dispatch primitives.

- Audited all callers: Phase 1 gen_servers are only used by tests and
REPL files (passthrough to Phase 2)
- Confirmed full primitive parity between Phase 1 and Phase 2 for both
interface pairs
- Created 4 sequenced child issues (BT-1108 through BT-1111) with
blocking relationships
- Updated ADR 0010 with migration status, caller analysis, and
completion criteria

## Migration Sequence

1. **BT-1108** (S): Switch REPL callers to Phase 2 primitives directly
2. **BT-1109** (M): Port Phase 1 unit tests to Phase 2 dispatch path
3. **BT-1110** (M, blocked by BT-1109): Delete `beamtalk_interface.erl`
4. **BT-1111** (M, blocked by BT-1108 + BT-1109): Delete
`beamtalk_workspace_interface.erl`

## Linear Issue

https://linear.app/beamtalk/issue/BT-1093

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

## Documentation

* Enhanced Architecture Decision Record documentation with detailed
migration planning, current system state overview, dependency analysis,
primitive function coverage comparison, and completion criteria for
internal system refactoring initiatives.

commit.message:
Prove h_decomp: source3 ≤ ε·(d/2) + max(0, d/2 - b_native)

Both LEFT and RIGHT child cases of the source3 halver+rank counting
argument are now fully proved. Key factorings to stay within heartbeats:

- fin_double_card_lt/ge: reusable card lemmas for Fin(2m) halves
- complement_counting_bound: abstract 4-step arithmetic shared by both cases
- source3_hard_case_tail: ε-arithmetic tail shared by both hard cases
- right_child_hard_case: full RIGHT complement argument as standalone lemma

One sorry remains: h_deficit (Seiferas Section 5 benchmark comparison).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: v0.7.0 — charter serve (MCP), docs preset content.adf, prefix collision fix

New features:
- charter serve: MCP server (stdio) exposing ADF context as tools/resources for
  Claude Code and other MCP clients. Tools: getProjectContext, getArchitecturalDecisions,
  getProjectState, getRecentChanges. Resources: adf://manifest, adf://modules/{name}.
- Static site detection (#30): isStaticWorkerSite() checks wrangler.toml [assets]
  config + no worker entrypoint → suggests docs preset instead of worker.
- docs preset content.adf (#31): bootstrap --preset docs now scaffolds content.adf
  (triggers: Markdown, MDX, frontmatter, Astro, navigation) alongside decisions.adf
  and planning.adf. MANIFEST_DOCS_SCAFFOLD updated with docs-relevant triggers.
- adf init and charter setup next-steps now mention adf bundle (#32).

Bug fix:
- Trigger prefix collision in contentToModule() (#36): changed from bare prefix
  \bauth to suffix-aware \bauth(?:s|ed|ing|ment|tion|ity|ication)?\b. Blocked
  "author", "authentic", "authority", "apiary" from matching short triggers.

Harness:
- Added 7 new edge-case scenarios (dedup-rephrased, dedup-partial-overlap,
  heading-dominates, auth-boundary, trigger-prefix-collision, large-injection,
  empty-heading). Full suite: 21/21 passing.
- harness/ directory committed (corpus, runner, evaluator, ollama, types).

All packages bumped 0.6.0 → 0.7.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: kill entire process tree to stop signal-cli JVM

signal-cli.bat spawns a Java JVM child. os.kill(pid, 9) only kills the
bat launcher; the JVM keeps running and holds the SQLite config lock,
causing the next startup to see "Config file is in use".

Switch to taskkill /F /T /PID for both orphan cleanup and normal stop,
which kills the bat + all child processes (including the JVM).

Also restore subscribeReceive (receive RPC is blocked in daemon mode)
with full exception envelope logging so we can inspect Note-to-Self data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(security): detect CVE-2025-1716 pickle bypass via pip.main() (#598)

* feat(security): detect CVE-2025-1716 pickle bypass via pip.main() callable

Add pip.main, pip._internal.main, pip._internal.cli.main.main, and
pip._vendor.distlib.scripts.ScriptMaker to ALWAYS_DANGEROUS_FUNCTIONS.
Add pip and pip._internal module prefixes to ALWAYS_DANGEROUS_MODULES.

This closes a picklescan bypass where attackers use pip.main() as the
callable in a pickle GLOBAL+REDUCE chain to install arbitrary packages
and execute code.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #598 review feedback

- Add _is_dangerous_module() helper for prefix-aware module matching
  so pip._internal.cli.main_parser is caught even when not explicitly
  listed
- Close temp files before scanning to avoid Windows file locking
- Add -> None return type hints on test methods
- Expand test assertions for all pip blocklist entries
- Add test for prefix matching behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply ruff formatting to test_pickle_scanner.py

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add comment-token bypass test and tighten pip assertion for CVE-2025-1716

- Add test_comment_token_does_not_bypass_pip_detection to verify that
  embedding a comment-like token in a malicious pickle payload does not
  suppress pip.main detection (per AGENTS.md CVE testing guidelines).
- Tighten test_pip_internal_main_detected to assert the issue message
  references "pip", not just that critical issues exist.
